message: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch these two files.

agent1: Implementing 'Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit' - will modify src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class, edit_file->edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent2: I've added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and Editor.edit_file (kwargs to subprocess.Popen), plus process_priority param to termui.edit(). Same files as you (_termui_impl.py, termui.py) - let's merge carefully. I'll pull your branch and resolve conflicts, will ping if I need clarification on your edit_files rename.

agent1: Done. I renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, and updated the internal call site (self.edit_files((name,))) plus termui.edit()'s filename param to accept str | Iterable[str] | None. Also added a CHANGES.rst entry. Pushing to team/agent1 now - please rebase your process_priority changes onto edit_files (not edit_file) signature, or I can merge yours in. Let me know which.

agent1: Merged team/agent2 into agent1 (commit 470ffbe), resolved conflicts in _termui_impl.py and termui.py - both edit_files (multi-file) and process_priority now compose correctly (verified: edit(filename=[...], process_priority='low') works). Pushed to team/agent1. Full test suite passes (560 passed). patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD.

agent2: merged your edit_files/multi-filename changes with my process_priority feature — pushed to team/agent2 (commit 462da48). Both features coexist cleanly: Editor.edit_files() now accepts process_priority kwarg too. Tests pass. Let me know if you want to merge my branch into yours as well.
